compaction.prompt:
You are performing STATE RECONSTRUCTION (not summarization) for a formal software development pipeline (v4.1). The pipeline has an Orchestrator coordinating subagents across cognitive stages (C2-C9) and operational stages (O1-O10). The orchestrator reconstructs full context from disk at every stage transition — your output needs only enough for it to know where it is, what to do next, and the current user interaction state.

OUTPUT FORMAT — produce a structured checkpoint, not prose:

## Pipeline State
- current_state: <from manifest>
- progress: <stage X/Y, module M/N if in O3>
- automode: <true/false>
- fast_track: <true/false, details if active>

## Phase Context
<see phase-specific rules below>

## Active User State
- Pending gate: <yes/no, which stage>
- Latest user instruction: <verbatim if present>
- Active correction loop: <origin stage if any>
- Unresolved escalation: <details if any>

## Next Action
- Next stage: <stage-id>
- Required input artifacts: <paths>
- Entry condition: <from routing table>

PHASE-SPECIFIC RULES — apply the section matching the current pipeline position:

[COGNITIVE PHASE — C2 through C9]
Preserve: current stage reasoning, user feedback on latest gate, key decisions affecting architecture direction, any C5 skip status. Discard: completed stage conversations (reports on disk), quoted artifact content, all dispatch/commit details.

[COGNITIVE-TO-OPERATIONAL BOUNDARY — C9 completed, entering O1]
This is the highest-value compaction point. Preserve ONLY: handoff verification status (pass/fail), total module count from task-graph, any user instructions for operational phase, automode/fast-track status. Discard: ALL cognitive stage conversations, reasoning, intermediate decisions — everything is encoded in committed artifacts (project-spec.md, architecture.md, implementation-plan.md, etc.). The operational phase needs zero cognitive context.

[O3 MODULE LOOP — O3_IN_PROGRESS]
Preserve: modules completed (list), modules remaining (list with order), current module status, any per-module issues or user instructions, cumulative error patterns if any. Discard: completed module conversations (per-module reports on disk), Builder return summaries for finished modules.

[POST-O3 — O3 completed, entering O4+]
Preserve ONLY: total modules generated, any known issues flagged during O3, module list for Validator context, user instructions. Discard: ALL per-module conversations, Builder summaries, O3 dispatch details — everything is in committed code and reports.

[VALIDATION/DEBUG — O4 through O6]
Preserve: current validation stage, findings summary, correction loop state (if active: originating stage, affected modules, correction notes), re-validation chain position. Discard: previous validation stage details if correction loop has moved past them.

[FINALIZATION — O7 through O10]
Preserve: current stage, CI status (if O8/O8.V), release version (if O9), any pending user gate. Discard: all prior stage conversations.

If the conversation contains a '## Pipeline Checkpoint' block, treat it as authoritative — preserve it verbatim as the foundation of your reconstruction. It was written by the orchestrator specifically for this purpose.

CRITICAL: never summarize a summary. If prior compacted content exists, preserve its structured data and merge with new information — do not re-summarize it.
